title: Top Five Will Smith Movies and the Bizarre Truth of 'Gemini Man'
format: Sean Fennessey and Amanda Dobbins dig into Ang Lee's 120-frames-per-second experiment 'Gemini Man,' then each run down their own five favorite Will Smith performances.

films.title: Gemini Man
films.note: An aging government assassin (Will Smith) is hunted by a younger clone of himself. Both agree the plot and dialogue are weak, but Sean thinks the motorcycle chase is one of the best action sequences he's seen, shot in Ang Lee's 120fps 4K 3D format, which no theater in the U.S. can actually project at full spec.

segments.heading: Ang Lee, De-Aging, and Whether Actors Still Matter
segments.note: the technology behind Gemini Man in context

films.title: Hulk
films.note: Sean points to this as a turning point where Lee started treating filmmaking as a technical experiment.

films.title: Billy Lynn's Long Halftime Walk
films.note: Lee's earlier 120fps drama, which Sean saw in a nearly empty theater.

films.title: Avatar
films.note: Sean's example of once-celebrated effects work that already looks dated on a TV screen.

films.title: Rogue One: A Star Wars Story
films.note: Cited for its digital recreation of Christopher Lee's Grand Moff Tarkin (Sean and Amanda briefly mix up which of the two Star Wars films had Tarkin versus the de-aged Carrie Fisher, and self-correct).

films.title: Dawn of the Planet of the Apes
films.note: Referenced alongside Gollum as prior motion-capture benchmarks for Junior's digital face.

films.title: The Irishman
films.note: This year's other big de-aging showcase, on Sean and Amanda's minds heading into awards season.

segments.heading: Top Five Will Smith Performances
segments.note: Sean and Amanda each run their own five, not a shared ranking

films.title: Bad Boys II
films.note: No. 5 on Sean's list, No. 4 on Amanda's. Both single out the Reggie interrogation scene as some of the funniest two minutes of Will Smith's career.

films.title: Focus
films.note: No. 5 on Amanda's list. Will Smith and Margot Robbie as con artists; a $160 million hit that Amanda thinks has already been forgotten.

films.title: Hitch
films.note: No. 4 on Sean's list, paired in conversation with Focus as a 'Will Smith charm' movie. Amanda doesn't have it on her list at all.

films.title: Independence Day
films.note: No. 2 on Amanda's list, kept out of the top spot only because Will Smith isn't in enough of the movie. She calls it the event of the universe when she was 13.

films.title: Ali
films.note: No. 2 on Sean's list. He calls it the one time Will Smith really let a director, Michael Mann, challenge him. Amanda tried rewatching it for her list and left it off.

films.title: Men in Black
films.note: No. 1 on Amanda's list. Sean never states a No. 1 of his own on tape.

segments.heading: More Will Smith, for Better or Worse

films.title: Six Degrees of Separation
films.note: A small, notable role: Will Smith plays a gay character, which Sean says he's never repeated.

films.title: Wild Wild West
films.note: A fiasco, per both hosts.

films.title: The Legend of Bagger Vance
films.note: Directed by Robert Redford, co-starring Matt Damon. Amanda remembers nothing about it, which she says is a mercy.

films.title: I, Robot
films.note: A massive hit Amanda calls completely forgettable; she was assigned it for a college science credit.

films.title: Hancock
films.note: Written by Vince Gilligan before Breaking Bad, released the same year as Iron Man.

films.title: The Pursuit of Happyness
films.note: His Oscar-nominated turn as a father trying to provide for his son.

films.title: I Am Legend
films.note: The one where he's alone with a dog, which Sean points to as the rare case of Will Smith carrying a movie solo.

films.title: Winter's Tale
films.note: He plays the devil. Amanda has never seen it.

films.title: Concussion
films.note: Part of a stretch of serious, awards-minded roles.

films.title: Suicide Squad
films.note: His attempt at a superhero franchise, which didn't work out.

films.title: Collateral Beauty
films.note: Sean wants to make it the first entry in a running 'disaster movie' segment.

films.title: Bright
films.note: One of the most-watched movies in Netflix history, per Sean, and not good.

films.title: Aladdin
films.note: His Genie sparked an ongoing disagreement: Amanda defends the character's flirtiness, Sean thinks it's wrong for a kids' movie.

films.title: Men in Black: International
films.note: Released without Will Smith this year, and Sean says it proved nobody else can sell the franchise.

films.title: Spies in Disguise
films.note: His upcoming animated feature.

films.title: Bad Boys for Life
films.note: The upcoming third Bad Boys movie.

films.title: King Richard
films.note: His then-upcoming role as Richard Williams, father of Serena and Venus Williams, which Sean and Amanda both peg as his best shot yet at an Oscar.

TV: The Fresh Prince of Bel-Air (Sean's No. 3 pick, specifically the Ben Vereen father-returns episode)
Web: Will Smith's Instagram videos (Amanda's No. 3 pick)
Promo: The Road Taken With CT and Bayo

Rogue One: A Star Wars Story.title: Rogue One: A Star Wars Story

Dawn of the Planet of the Apes.title: Dawn of the Planet of the Apes
